clusters.title: Window rollover can race with concurrent sample recording

member_findings.title: Window rollover can race with concurrent sample recording
member_findings.impact: RecordUrmaResult publishes the new windowStartTimeMs_ with compare_exchange before exchanging successCount_ and failureCount_. A concurrent request that observes the new start can record into what it believes is the new window, but the rollover thread then exchanges those counters into the expired-window snapshot and clears them. Under concurrent Set/Get traffic this can make the previous window appear to meet min_sample_count or success-rate thresholds using samples from the next window, or lose/move new-window samples, causing spurious worker switches or missed URMA failover decisions.
member_findings.fix: Make window settlement and sample recording atomic with respect to each other. For example, protect the start/counter rollover and RecordInCurrentWindow with a mutex, or introduce a settling state/generation so no thread can record into the new window until the old counters have been snapshotted and reset.

member_findings.title: Remove committed AI conversation transcript
member_findings.impact: This new hidden repository artifact is not source, test data, or durable documentation. It embeds an implementation transcript that can go stale immediately and mislead future maintainers about what code was actually reviewed or intended.
member_findings.fix: Drop the .conversations file from the PR, or move any durable design rationale into the normal repo documentation or code comments near the relevant implementation.

clusters.title: Avoid URMA-specific no-op hooks on the generic worker API

member_findings.title: Avoid URMA-specific no-op hooks on the generic worker API
member_findings.impact: The common client-worker interface now exposes URMA data-plane tracking and switch callbacks even for implementations that cannot use them, where the default behavior is a silent no-op. That makes the abstraction leaky and gives future call sites no compile-time signal that the operation only has meaning for remote URMA-capable workers.
member_findings.fix: Keep the URMA failover contract on the remote worker API or a small capability-specific interface, and wire callers through that narrower type instead of adding generic no-op methods to IClientWorkerCommonApi.

clusters.title: Disabled failover still resets shared atomics on every URMA result

member_findings.title: Disabled failover still resets shared atomics on every URMA result
member_findings.impact: When urma_failover_success_rate_ratio is set to 0.0, the feature is documented as disabled, but every hot Set/Get URMA result still enters RecordUrmaResult and calls ResetWindow. That performs several release atomic stores on the per-worker tracker for every request, so a high-throughput client that disables failover still pays shared cache-line traffic and contention on the foreground data path.
member_findings.fix: Make the disabled path a true no-op after state has been cleared, for example by clearing only on the transition to disabled or by having callers skip RecordUrmaDataPlaneResult while the ratio is 0.0.

clusters.title: Missing tests for new URMA failover flag validators

member_findings.title: Missing tests for new URMA failover flag validators
member_findings.impact: The PR adds runtime validation for the operator-facing `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` and `urma_failover_min_sample_count` flags, but the added tests only use valid values. Regressions that accept NaN, out-of-range ratios, or a zero sample count would not be caught, and those invalid hot-update values can silently disable or distort failover behavior.
member_findings.fix: Add focused flag-validation tests, for example in `tests/ut/common/util/flags_test.cpp`, that verify ratio accepts 0.0 and 1.0 but rejects negative, greater-than-one, and non-finite values, and that min sample count rejects 0 while accepting positive values through the same flag update path used by client config monitoring.
